headline: IEA describes Hormuz disruption as largest supply crisis in oil market history
body: The IEA describes the Strait of Hormuz crisis as 'the largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.' Flows through the Strait fell from ~20 million b/d pre-war to an average of 2.7 million b/d in March-May 2026. A US-Iran deal was announced last week aiming to reopen the Strait. UAE's Habshan-Fujairah pipeline bypass is operating at 1.8 million b/d capacity. Some UAE tankers have been observed with transponders turned off to avoid detection.
